methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
NuScale Power is a public-stage nuclear energy company that has raised over $600 million to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of eight firms across the nuclear sector: three publicly traded peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and five private-stage companies (Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, X-Energy, Fuse Energy, and Terrestrial Energy).

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable density within narrow filters. The nuclear energy sector contains only four same-sector peers total, with fewer than three firms meeting simultaneous criteria for stage, valuation tier, and development profile. A forced peer-median calculation using mismatched cohorts would introduce unacceptable bias; therefore this method was excluded from triangulation.

The analysis cannot proceed to a synthesized valuation range without additional methodological input (DCF, revenue multiple, or asset-based approaches) not present in the provided dataset. Key uncertainties constraining valuation include: technology maturation and regulatory pathway for small modular reactor deployment, competitive positioning within a fragmented private nuclear cohort, and execution risk tied to capital intensity and long development timelines. The public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) trade under distinct liquidity and disclosure regimes that may not anchor private-market value. Without quantitative results from alternative methods, only the data point that NuScale has mobilized $600M+ in capital can be stated with certainty.
